item.text: Using the graph-only path, I’ll query Orbit graph tools from the repo root to locate `LoopAuditEvent` and then extract the exact enum variant identifiers from its declaration before sorting them alphabetically.

Execute a tool

Usage: orbit tool run [OPTIONS] <NAME>

Arguments:
  <NAME>  Tool name

Options:
      --input <INPUT>            JSON input for the tool (use --input-file to avoid shell escaping issues with rich content)
      --root <ROOT>              Override the Orbit root directory (highest precedence)
      --input-file <INPUT_FILE>  Path to a JSON file to use as input (bypasses shell escaping; preferred for markdown or multi-line content)
      --agent <AGENT>            Explicit agent name for provenance attribution (overrides ORBIT_AGENT_NAME)
      --model <MODEL>            Explicit agent model for provenance attribution (overrides ORBIT_AGENT_MODEL)
      --timeout <TIMEOUT>        Execution timeout (e.g. "30s", "5000ms")
      --dry-run                  Validate without executing
      --fields <FIELDS>          Comma-separated top-level fields to keep from object output
      --full                     Return the tool's full unfiltered JSON output
      --pretty                   Pretty-print JSON output for human debugging
      --output <OUTPUT>          Output format [default: json] [possible values: json, text]
  -h, --help                     Print help

Manage and run Orbit tools, including external MCP plugins

Usage: orbit tool [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  list      List all registered tools
  show      Show detailed information about a tool
  run       Execute a tool
  add       Register an external tool or MCP plugin
  scaffold  Generate a starter external tool plugin
  remove    Remove an external tool
  enable    Enable a disabled tool
  disable   Disable a tool
  doctor    Validate tool health

Options:
      --root <ROOT>  Override the Orbit root directory (highest precedence)
  -h, --help         Print help

Examples:
  orbit tool scaffold ./plugins/hello_orbit.py --name demo.hello
  orbit tool add ./plugins/hello_orbit.py
  orbit tool show demo.hello

item.text: I hit a read-only database error on `orbit tool show`, so I’m switching to direct `orbit tool run orbit.graph.search` calls and iterating input shape until we get a valid graph result for `LoopAuditEvent`.

item.text: I’ve retrieved the enum source from `orbit.graph.show`; now I’m running one more shell pipeline to extract just the variant identifiers and sort them alphabetically to match your required output format exactly.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc "orbit tool run orbit.graph.show --input '{\"selector\":\"symbol:crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/audit/mod.rs#LoopAuditEvent:struct\"}' | jq -r '.source' | awk 'match("'$0,/''^[[:space:]]*([A-Z][A-Za-z0-9_]*)[[:space:]]*('"\\{|\\(|,)/,m){print m[1]}' | sort"

/bin/zsh -lc "orbit tool run orbit.graph.show --input '{\"selector\":\"symbol:crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/audit/mod.rs#LoopAuditEvent:struct\"}' | jq -r '.source' | awk 'match("'$0,/''^[[:space:]]*([A-Z][A-Za-z0-9_]*)[[:space:]]*('"\\{|\\(|,)/,m){print m[1]}' | sort"

/bin/zsh -lc "orbit tool run orbit.graph.show --input '{\"selector\":\"symbol:crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/audit/mod.rs#LoopAuditEvent:struct\"}' | jq -r '.source' | awk '/"'^[[:space:]]*[A-Z][A-Za-z0-9_]*[[:space:]]*('"\\{|\\(|,)/ { line="'$0; gsub(/''^[[:space:]]*/,"",line); sub(/[[:space:]]*('"\\{|\\(|,).*/,\"\",line); print line }' | sort"

/bin/zsh -lc "orbit tool run orbit.graph.show --input '{\"selector\":\"symbol:crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/audit/mod.rs#LoopAuditEvent:struct\"}' | jq -r '.source' | awk '/"'^[[:space:]]*[A-Z][A-Za-z0-9_]*[[:space:]]*('"\\{|\\(|,)/ { line="'$0; gsub(/''^[[:space:]]*/,"",line); sub(/[[:space:]]*('"\\{|\\(|,).*/,\"\",line); print line }' | sort"
